skill.name: schema-markup
skill.description: Create, audit, and optimize schema.org structured data for web pages using JSON-LD patterns and validation guidance.
skill.summary: Generate accurate JSON-LD schema markup for common web page types.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, network, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are markdown examples and SEO guidance text. No executable scripts, command invocation, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer treated markdown backticks and code fences as Ruby shell execution. These lines are inline HTML guidance or fenced JSON and JSX examples, not executable shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows markdown formatting and example blocks only. No shell command, Ruby code, or command execution API is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Flags Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The URL findings are schema.org contexts, placeholder example.com values, social profile examples, and validator links. They are not used for network requests or data transmission.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear inside JSON-LD examples or validation resource bullets. The skill contains no code path that performs outbound requests.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The high-severity cryptography alerts are triggered by ordinary schema terms such as description fields and JSON-LD guidance. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose or JSON-LD property names. There is no semantic evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Flags Are Schema Guidance Terms
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to SEO guidance, address properties, invalid value advice, and CMS implementation notes. They do not instruct system or network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is structured data implementation guidance. No scanning, discovery, enumeration, or host inspection behavior is described.

content.value_statement: Schema markup is easy to get wrong, which can block rich result eligibility. This skill helps create accurate JSON-LD and validation checklists for common page types.
actual_capabilities: Recommends schema types for common page categories, including articles, products, FAQs, events, and local businesses.
Creates JSON-LD implementation guidance with placement instructions for pages and templates.
Explains required and recommended properties for common schema.org types.
Supports multi-schema page planning with graph-style structured data patterns.
Provides validation guidance using Google Rich Results Test, Schema.org Validator, and Search Console.
Asks for page type, rich result goals, available data, existing schema, and technology stack.
limitations: It does not run live validators or inspect a website by itself.
Placeholder URLs and example values must be replaced before publishing.
It cannot guarantee rich results because search engines decide eligibility.
It focuses on structured data, not complete SEO strategy or technical SEO audits.

use_cases.title: Add Schema to Product Pages
use_cases.description: Create structured data for product name, offers, availability, brand, and visible review details.

use_cases.title: Improve Editorial Rich Result Eligibility
use_cases.description: Build article schema that reflects headlines, authors, dates, images, publishers, and canonical page identity.

use_cases.title: Standardize Structured Data Templates
use_cases.description: Plan reusable schema patterns for CMS, WordPress, React, or Next.js page templates.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the Right Schema Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this page description and recommend the best schema type. Explain required fields and any rich result limits.

prompt_templates.title: Create Page Schema
prompt_templates.prompt: Create JSON-LD schema markup for this page using the visible content I provide. Include placement and testing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Markup
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing structured data and identify errors, mismatches with page content, missing properties, and validation risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Schema Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable schema strategy for these page templates. Include shared organization data, breadcrumbs, and validation workflow.

output_examples.input: A SaaS product page with pricing, screenshots, and customer ratings.
output_examples.output: A SoftwareApplication schema plan with required properties, offer details, rating cautions, placement notes, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: A blog post with author, publish date, modified date, hero image, and publisher logo.
output_examples.output: An Article schema recommendation that maps each visible page element to the correct structured data property.

output_examples.input: A local business location page with address, hours, phone number, and service area.
output_examples.output: A LocalBusiness schema checklist covering address, hours, contact data, images, and content consistency checks.

best_practices: Only mark up content that is visible and accurate on the page.
Validate every implementation before release and monitor Search Console after deployment.
Use reusable templates for repeated page types so schema stays consistent.
anti_patterns: Do not add ratings or reviews that are not visible on the page.
Do not copy placeholder URLs, names, or dates into production markup.
Do not use schema markup to describe content that the page does not contain.

faq.question: What schema formats does this skill prefer?
faq.answer: It prefers JSON-LD because Google recommends it and it is easier to maintain.

faq.question: Can it help with product schema?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers product names, images, offers, availability, brands, ratings, and review cautions.

faq.question: Does it validate schema automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives validation steps for Google Rich Results Test, Schema.org Validator, and Search Console.

faq.question: Can it create schema for multiple page types?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers articles, products, FAQ pages, how-to content, breadcrumbs, events, and local businesses.

faq.question: Will schema markup guarantee rich snippets?
faq.answer: No. Correct markup improves eligibility, but search engines decide when rich results appear.

faq.question: What information should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the page type, visible content, target rich result, existing schema, and your website technology stack.
